mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 9.0% indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-stat below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in new home sales and housing starts signal strengthening residential demand that boosts construction and durable goods sectors, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a statistically significant positive return differential with a p-value above 0.05 compared to baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of stable monetary policy reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization that supports near-term equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during stable policy periods produce negative or flat forward returns, or the signal Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above 0.5% MoM are followed by positive equity returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal out-of-sample hit rate drops below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates deteriorating labor market quality that erodes consumer confidence and corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a positive return differential or fails to reject the null hypothesis of zero predictive alpha in a 5-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects expanding aggregate demand that directly lifts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth periods show no statistically significant positive return bias with a t-stat below 1.0, or the signal rank correlation with forward returns remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.8% signals acute supply-side contraction that disrupts manufacturing output and supply chains, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops below -0.8% MoM are followed by positive equity returns in more than 50% of instances, or the signal predictive accuracy falls below random chance in out-of-sample testing.
